title: Dinebra paspaloides
intro: Dinebra paspaloides, also known as Chinese timothy grass or wild rye, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, Dinebra paspaloides has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Dinebra paspaloides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Dinebra paspaloides is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders, as well as to boost the immune system.
